title: veronica cuneifolia var. atlantica
intro: Veronica cuneifolia var. atlantica, also known as the Atlantic speedwell or Atlantic brookweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the figwort family, Native to the western Atlantic region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Veronica cuneifolia var. atlantica include its potential to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
